skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Use CodeRabbit CLI to review changed code, summarize findings by severity, and guide fix-and-review cycles.
skill.summary: Runs CodeRabbit reviews for changed code and organizes actionable feedback.
skill.author: coderabbitai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-like patterns, hardcoded URLs, filesystem redirection, and weak-crypto indicators. Review found the weak-crypto findings to be false positives from Markdown text, but confirmed that the skill intentionally runs an external CLI and sends code diffs to CodeRabbit, so users need a data-sharing warning.

medium_findings.title: Code Diff Data Sent to Third-Party Service
medium_findings.description: The skill states that the CodeRabbit CLI sends code diffs to the CodeRabbit API. This is expected for the skill, but it can expose proprietary code or secrets if users review sensitive changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents that code diffs are transmitted to the CodeRabbit API. The same lines warn users to avoid secrets, which confirms the data exposure risk is known and contextual.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Required for Review Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run coderabbit or cr commands. The commands are legitimate and mostly fixed, but they execute local tooling and depend on the installed CLI and authentication state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill. There is no evidence of command injection or malicious payloads, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Benign Shell Redirection in Prerequisite Check
low_findings.description: The /dev/null reference is used to suppress version-check output when testing whether CodeRabbit CLI is installed. This is a standard shell pattern and not a sensitive file access pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The redirection appears only in a CLI version check and does not read or write user data. The surrounding lines show a normal installation prerequisite check.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain the skill description, a command flag mention, and a Markdown table header.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and Markdown, not hash functions or cryptographic operations. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or other weak crypto usage in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires running local CodeRabbit CLI commands to inspect changes. This is expected behavior, but users should verify the CLI source and avoid executing commands copied from review output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are directly present in fenced examples. The skill includes safeguards about official installation and untrusted output, reducing but not removing the operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Processing Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on CodeRabbit API processing of code diffs. This should be disclosed because it can move repository content outside the local workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network data flow is explicitly documented by the skill. The risk is clear when reviewed code contains secrets, credentials, or private source code.

content.value_statement: Manual review can miss defects, regressions, and security issues in changed code. This skill runs CodeRabbit CLI reviews and turns the results into prioritized fixes.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether CodeRabbit CLI is installed and authenticated.
Runs CodeRabbit reviews for all, committed, or uncommitted changes.
Supports branch and commit comparisons through CodeRabbit CLI flags.
Groups review feedback into critical, warning, and info categories.
Guides iterative fix, rerun, and verification cycles for code changes.
limitations: Requires CodeRabbit CLI to be installed from a trusted source.
Requires CodeRabbit authentication before reviews can run.
Sends code diffs to CodeRabbit API for analysis.
Does not replace human review for architecture, policy, or compliance decisions.

use_cases.title: Pre-PR Review
use_cases.description: Run a focused review before opening a pull request and fix important issues early.

use_cases.title: Security-Focused Change Check
use_cases.description: Find risky code paths, data handling mistakes, and potential vulnerabilities in modified files.

use_cases.title: Automated Fix Verification
use_cases.description: Review changes, apply fixes, and rerun CodeRabbit until only acceptable findings remain.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a standard review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current code changes with CodeRabbit and summarize the critical, warning, and info findings.

prompt_templates.title: Review Uncommitted Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused local review before staging or committing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use CodeRabbit to review only my uncommitted changes. Create a prioritized task list from the results.

prompt_templates.title: Review Against Main
prompt_templates.scenario: Pull request preparation against a target branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my changes against the main branch with CodeRabbit. Highlight blockers first, then quality improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Fix and Verify Findings
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced autonomous review and remediation cycle.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a CodeRabbit review, fix critical and warning findings, then rerun the review until the remaining issues are acceptable.

output_examples.input: Review my uncommitted changes before I create a pull request.
output: Critical: No confirmed critical issues were reported.
Warning: Two logic risks need changes before review.
Info: Three style suggestions can be handled later.

output_examples.input: Run CodeRabbit and help me fix the important findings.
output: The review found one warning about missing error handling.
I added the missing failure path and reran the review.
The follow-up review no longer reports that warning.

output_examples.input: Check my branch against main for security issues.
output: Security review completed against the target branch.
One finding needs manual confirmation because it touches authentication flow.
No secrets should be present in the reviewed diff before sharing it with CodeRabbit.

best_practices: Inspect diffs for secrets before sending them to CodeRabbit API.
Install CodeRabbit CLI from an official package manager or verified release.
Treat review output as advice and verify fixes with tests when possible.
anti_patterns: Do not run reviews on diffs that contain credentials or private keys.
Do not execute commands from review output without explicit user approval.
Do not treat info-level suggestions as blockers without project context.

faq.question: Does this skill require an external tool?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires CodeRabbit CLI and a valid authenticated session.

faq.question: Does the skill send code outside my machine?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that CodeRabbit CLI sends code diffs to the CodeRabbit API.

faq.question: Can it review only uncommitted changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a workflow for reviewing uncommitted changes with CodeRabbit CLI.

faq.question: Can it compare against a branch?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes branch comparison through the CodeRabbit base branch option.

faq.question: Does it automatically fix every issue?
faq.answer: No. It can guide fixes, but users should verify behavior and run relevant tests.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning about external command execution and third-party diff processing.
